methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $300M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $350M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
GrayMatter Robotics is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Ariyan Kabir and Brual Shah, currently valued at $300M+ post-money on $70M+ in total raised. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of nine private frontier-tech robotics firms at similar stage, plus one public-market reference point.

Two triangulation methods apply here. Stage-progression analysis, using the $300M last post-money as a base and applying a typical 1.9x Series B-to-next-stage multiplier, implies $570M. Peer-median analysis across five same-sector, same-stage comparables—1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), and Fairmat ($350M)—yields a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range from $350M to $10.0B.

These methods triangulate to a valuation range of $484M to $1.9B, with a median of $1.1B. The wide dispersion reflects material heterogeneity within Series B robotics valuations, particularly the outsized $10.0B valuation of 1X Technologies, which may reflect differentiated market positioning. Key uncertainties include GrayMatter's relative competitive positioning versus the peer set, unit economics trajectory, and whether comparable multiples will persist through the next funding round. The current $300M valuation sits near the lower quartile of the derived range, suggesting either discount-to-peers or undervaluation depending on GrayMatter's relative execution metrics and market opportunity sizing.
